code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence. Uses predicate‑specific phrasing when possible
    and combines all clauses into one natural‑language sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates.
    # The placeholder {subject} and {object} will be substituted.
    templates = {
        "almaMater": "{subject} studied at {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
        "birthName": "{subject}'s birth name was {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "dateOfRetirement": "{subject} retired in {object}",
        "occupation": "{subject} worked as a {object}",
        "status": "{subject} is {object}",
        "timeInSpace": "{subject} spent {object} minutes in space",
        "mission": "{subject} flew on the mission {object}",
        "selectedByNasa": "{subject} was selected by NASA in {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} was a citizen of {object}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "{subject} served as chief of the astronaut office in {object}",
        "title": "{subject} held the title of {object}",
        "ribbonAward": "{subject} received the ribbon award {object}",
        "operator": "{subject} was operated by {object}",
        "backupPilot": "{object} was the backup pilot for {subject}",
        "commander": "{object} commanded {subject}",
        "crewMembers": "{object} was a crew member of {subject}",
        "representative": "{subject} was represented by {object}",
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "awards": "{subject} received {object} awards",
        "award": "{subject} received the award {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "fossil": "{subject} is known for the fossil {object}",
        "senators": "{subject} is represented by senator {object}",
        "part": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "bird": "{subject}'s state bird is {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "affiliation": "{subject} is affiliated with {object}",
        "competeIn": "{subject} competes in {object}",
        "president": "{subject}'s president is {object}",
        "gemstone": "{subject}'s state gemstone is {object}",
        "mascot": "{subject}'s mascot is {object}",
        "cosparId": "{subject} has COSPAR ID {object}",
        "utcOffset": "{subject} has UTC offset {object}",
        "higher": "{object} is higher than {subject}",
        "bird": "{subject}'s state bird is {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject for better entity linking
    subject_groups = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_groups:
            subject_groups[t.subject] = []
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build sentence parts with intelligent clause linking
    sentence_parts = []

    for subject, group_triples in subject_groups.items():
        clauses = []
        for t in group_triples:
            obj = t.object.strip().strip('"')

            # Special handling for timeInSpace to convert minutes to hours for readability
            if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
                try:
                    minutes = float(obj.replace('"', '').replace("'", ""))
                    hours = minutes / 60
                    if hours.is_integer():
                        obj = f"{int(hours)} hours"
                    else:
                        obj = f"{hours:.1f} hours"
                except ValueError:
                    pass

            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=obj)
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
                clause = f"{t.subject} {pred} {obj}"
            clauses.append((t.predicate, clause))

        # Combine clauses for this subject with relative pronouns when possible
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence_parts.append(clauses[0][1])
        elif len(clauses) == 2:
            # Use relative pronouns for better flow
            main_clause = clauses[0][1]
            second_clause = clauses[1][1]
            # Try to use "who", "which", or "and" based on context
            if "was born" in main_clause or "studied at" in main_clause:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{main_clause}, who {second_clause.split(' ', 1)[1] if second_clause.startswith(subject) else second_clause}")
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{main_clause} and {second_clause}")
        else:
            # Create more complex sentence with relative clauses
            main_clause = clauses[0][1]
            relative_clauses = []
            for pred, clause in clauses[1:]:
                # Use "who" for people, "which" for things, "where" for places
                if any(phrase in clause for phrase in ["was born", "studied", "worked", "retired", "died"]):
                    relative_clauses.append(f"who {clause.split(' ', 1)[1] if clause.startswith(subject) else clause}")
                elif any(phrase in clause for phrase in ["flew", "mission", "crew", "pilot", "commanded"]):
                    relative_clauses.append(f"and {clause}")
                else:
                    relative_clauses.append(f"with {clause.split(' ', 1)[1] if clause.startswith(subject) else clause}")

            if relative_clauses:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{main_clause}, {' '.join(relative_clauses)}")
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{main_clause}, {' and '.join([c[1] for c in clauses[1:]])}")

    # Combine all subject groups into one sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0]
    elif len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        sentence = f"{sentence_parts[0]}, and {sentence_parts[1]}"
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(sentence_parts[:-1]) + f", and {sentence_parts[-1]}"

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
